OB/GYN Clinical Notes

All notes

Foundation · Anatomy
OB 00
OB/GYN Anatomy & Embryology

Uterine supports, blood supply, ureter relationships, measurements, and embryology.

Cardinal ligament · Ureter · Müllerian ducts↗
Emergency · OB
OB 01
Postpartum Haemorrhage

The leading cause of maternal mortality. The 4 Ts, why BP is a late sign, and how to escalate without hesitation.

4 Ts · Atony · Emergency↗
Antenatal · OB
OB 02
Preeclampsia

Not a blood pressure problem. A disease of the placenta that raises BP. The distinction changes how you manage it entirely.

HTN · HELLP · Magnesium↗
Emergency · OB
OB 03
Ectopic Pregnancy

Ectopic until proven otherwise. The triad, the discriminatory zone, and what negative urine hCG actually rules out.

hCG · Rupture · Ultrasound↗
Antenatal · OB
OB 04
Placenta Praevia

Painless bright red bleeding in the third trimester. Why placental position determines route of delivery — and why you never do a VE.

APH · Accreta · Caesarean↗
Antenatal · OB
OB 05
Gestational Diabetes

Why hyperglycaemia in pregnancy endangers both mother and baby. The OGTT, thresholds, and why macrosomia is not just a big baby.

OGTT · Macrosomia · Insulin↗
Emergency · OB
OB 06
Shoulder Dystocia

The head delivers. The shoulders don't follow. Five minutes. HELPERR must be automatic — not recalled under pressure.

HELPERR · McRoberts · Drill↗
Emergency · OB
OB 07
Preterm Labour

Labour before 37 weeks. Why you give steroids before tocolysis, and why magnesium at 28 weeks is not about seizures.

Tocolysis · Steroids · Neuroprotection↗
Early Pregnancy · OB
OB 08
Miscarriage

Pregnancy loss before 24 weeks. The types, what the ultrasound tells you, and why management is a shared decision — not a protocol.

ERPC · Misoprostol · Anti-D↗
Emergency · OB
OB 09
Cord Prolapse

Cord before presenting part. You have minutes. The position that saves the cord until theatre is not optional — it is the intervention.

CTG · Category 1 CS · Manual↗
Emergency · OB
OB 10
Placental Abruption

Premature separation of a normally sited placenta. Painful APH, fetal distress, coagulopathy, and the concealed bleed that hides the true loss.

APH · DIC · Concealed↗
Labour · OB
OB 11
Induction of Labour

IOL at 41 weeks. Bishop score, cervical ripening, syntocinon. Why failed IOL is not a caesarean — it's a clinical decision.

Bishop · Syntocinon · Prostaglandins↗
Antenatal · OB
OB 12
Obstetric Cholestasis

Intractable itch in the third trimester, worst at night, no rash. Bile acids rise. Stillbirth risk rises with them.

Bile Acids · Ursodeoxycholic · IOL↗
Gynaecology · GYN
GYN 01
Ovarian Cysts

Functional vs pathological. The RMI, which features demand surgery, and how torsion presents before the classic signs appear.

RMI · CA-125 · Torsion↗
Oncology · GYN
GYN 02
Cervical Cancer

HPV first, then CIN, then cancer. The screening pathway, treatment of CIN, and the one symptom that must never be attributed to a cervical ectropion without investigation.

HPV · LLETZ · FIGO↗
Gynaecology · GYN
GYN 03
Endometriosis

Cyclical pain that isn't treated. The diagnostic delay, the laparoscopy that confirms it, and why suppression isn't the same as cure.

Laparoscopy · Dysmenorrhoea · Fertility↗
Endocrine · GYN
GYN 04
PCOS

Rotterdam criteria. Insulin resistance, androgen excess, anovulation. Lifestyle is first-line — not metformin, not OCP.

Rotterdam · Metformin · Anovulation↗
Gynaecology · GYN
GYN 05
Fibroids

The most common benign pelvic tumour. Submucosal bleeds, intramural bulk, the fibroid that causes infertility — and who actually needs surgery.

HMB · Myomectomy · UAE↗
Infectious · GYN
GYN 06
Pelvic Inflammatory Disease

Ascending infection. Chlamydia + gonorrhoea + anaerobes. The tubo-ovarian abscess that needs drainage — and why treating early preserves fertility.

TOA · NAAT · Empirical ABx↗
Endocrine · GYN
GYN 07
Menopause

Twelve months without a period. HRT — who benefits, who it harms, and why the WHI changed prescribing for a decade based on misapplied data.

HRT · WHI · Vasomotor↗
Dermatology · GYN
GYN 08
Vulval Conditions

Lichen sclerosus, lichen planus, VIN. The conditions missed, the biopsy delayed, and the transformation that could have been caught earlier.

Lichen Sclerosus · VIN · Biopsy↗
Oncology · GYN
GYN 09
Ovarian Cancer

The silent killer that isn't silent — the symptoms are there, they're just vague. Why stage at diagnosis determines everything.

CA-125 · FIGO · BRCA · Debulking↗
Oncology · GYN
GYN 16
Ovarian Tumors

Epithelial, germ cell, sex cord-stromal, and metastatic tumors — classification, markers, and clinical features.

Dermoid · Krukenberg · Granulosa cell · AFP · hCG↗
Reproductive · GYN
GYN 10
Subfertility

One in seven couples. The structured investigation, when to start, and why IVF is the end of the pathway — not the beginning.

Semen Analysis · IVF · Tubal · Anovulation↗
Oncology · GYN
GYN 11
Endometrial Cancer

The most common gynaecological cancer. Postmenopausal bleeding is the alarm symptom — and 90% of women present because of it. Early by design.

PMB · Type I/II · Hysterectomy↗
Urogynaecology · GYN
GYN 12
Urinary Incontinence

Stress vs urgency — the mechanism, the investigation, and why surgery before conservative treatment is always the wrong sequence.

Urodynamics · TVT · Bladder Training↗
Reproductive · GYN
GYN 13
Contraception

Pearl index, UKMEC, reversibility. Why the most effective methods are the ones least frequently offered first.

LARC · UKMEC · Emergency↗
Infectious · GYN
GYN 14
STIs

Chlamydia, gonorrhoea, syphilis, herpes. The presentations, the tests, the treatments — and why partner notification is part of the prescription.

NAAT · Syphilis · Herpes · Contact Tracing↗
Emergency · GYN
GYN 15
Acute Gynaecology

Ovarian torsion, TOA, acute vulval haematoma. The time-critical diagnoses — and the detail that makes the difference between ovary saved and ovary lost.

Torsion · TOA · Bartholin's↗
Oncology · OB
OB A
Gestational Trophoblastic Disease

Molar pregnancy to choriocarcinoma. β-hCG as the perfect tumour marker. Why GTD is one of the most curable cancers in medicine.

GTD · Molar · Choriocarcinoma · β-hCG↗
Haematology · OB
OB B
Anaemia in Pregnancy

Iron deficiency, folate, B12, sickle cell, thalassaemia. Ferritin targets, IV iron, and who gets 5 mg folic acid.

IDA · Ferritin · Haemoglobinopathy↗
Postnatal · OB
OB C
The Puerperium

Lochia, involution, puerperal sepsis, VTE risk, baby blues vs postnatal depression vs postpartum psychosis.

Lochia · Sepsis · Mental health · VTE↗
Haematology · OB
OB D
Rhesus Disease

Anti-D prophylaxis, sensitising events, Kleihauer test, and haemolytic disease of the newborn.

Anti-D · HDFN · Kleihauer · RAADP↗
High Risk · OB
OB E
Multiple Pregnancy

Chorionicity — DCDA, MCDA, MCMA. Twin-to-twin transfusion syndrome, laser ablation, and why one placenta doubles the risk.

TTTS · Chorionicity · Lambda · T-sign↗
First Trimester · OB
OB F
Hyperemesis Gravidarum

The vomiting that becomes an emergency. Wernicke's risk, antiemetic ladder, and why you never give glucose before thiamine.

HG · Wernicke's · Thiamine · Ondansetron↗
Intrapartum · OB
OB G
CTG Interpretation

Baseline, variability, accelerations, decelerations. Category 1–2–3. The sinusoidal pattern that means emergency.

CTG · BRAVE · Deceleration · FBS↗
Intrapartum · OB
OB H
Normal Labour

Stages, latent phase, partogram, 3 Ps, mechanisms. When to wait and when to intervene.

Partogram · Latent phase · ARM · Syntocinon↗
Malpresentation · OB
OB I
Breech Presentation

Frank, complete, footling. ECV at 36 weeks. Vaginal breech — hands off until umbilicus.

ECV · Burns-Marshall · Footling · Term Breech Trial↗
Antenatal · OB
OB J
Antenatal Screening

Combined test, NIPT, nuchal translucency. T21, T18, T13. Screening generates a risk — not a diagnosis.

T21 · NIPT · Nuchal · CVS · Amniocentesis↗
Mental Health · OB
OB K
Psychiatric Disorders in Pregnancy

Postpartum psychosis, PND, valproate contraindication. Suicide is the leading indirect maternal death.

EPDS · Psychosis · Valproate · MBU↗
Antenatal · OB
OB L
Fetal Growth

SFH every appointment. SGA vs FGR. Reversed end-diastolic flow = deliver today.

SFH · SGA · FGR · REDF · Doppler↗
Antenatal · OB
OB M
Amniotic Fluid

Polyhydramnios vs oligohydramnios. DVP preferred over AFI. Potter sequence. Absent stomach bubble = oesophageal atresia.

DVP · AFI · Potter · Oesophageal atresia↗
Foundations · OB
OB N
Physiology of Pregnancy

CO ↑40%, plasma volume ↑50%, GFR ↑50%. Creatinine 80 in pregnancy is abnormal. Left lateral tilt first.

Cardiac output · GFR · Aortocaval · D-dimer↗
Infection · OB
OB O
Infection in Pregnancy

TORCH, GBS, varicella, parvovirus. CMV is the most common congenital infection. GBS policy — risk-based, not universal screen.

TORCH · GBS · CMV · Parvovirus · VZV↗
Intrapartum · OB
OB P
Episiotomy & Perineal Tears

OASIS classification 1–4. Mediolateral at 60°. PR examination mandatory after every vaginal delivery.

OASIS · Mediolateral · PR exam · Repair↗
Gynaecology · GYN
GYN A
Congenital Anomalies of the Genital Tract

Müllerian anomalies, MRKH, AIS, imperforate hymen. Septate most common. Always scan the kidneys.

MRKH · Septate · Imperforate hymen · AIS · Renal↗
